properties.displayName: CISA KEV Detected on Device - MDVM Correlation (DISABLED - hunting only)
properties.description: DISABLED BY DESIGN. MDVM already surfaces CISA KEV-tagged CVEs in its built-in recommendations dashboard, so this rule does not need to generate Sentinel incidents. The KQL is preserved for ad-hoc hunting, workbook reuse, and as a turn-key option if a future deployment wants paging-grade KEV alerts. To re-enable: set 'enabled' to true and 'incidentConfiguration.createIncident' to true. Credits: Cyberlorians (TVM ingestion pattern) and Matt Zorich / kqlquery.com (KEV correlation).
properties.query:
let PreviousIncidents = SecurityIncident
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
    | where Title has_all ("CVE-", "CISA KEV")
    | distinct Title;
let ActiveExceptions = materialize(
    union isfuzzy=true
        (_GetWatchlist('KEV-Exceptions')
            | extend Exp = todatetime(tostring(ExpirationDate))
            | where isnull(Exp) or Exp > now()
            | extend ExceptionKey = strcat(tostring(DeviceName), '|', tostring(CveId))
            | project ExceptionKey),
        (datatable(ExceptionKey:string)[])
    | distinct ExceptionKey
);
let CISA_KEV = externaldata(
    cveID: string,
    vendorProject: string,
    product: string,
    vulnerabilityName: string,
    dateAdded: datetime,
    shortDescription: string,
    requiredAction: string,
    dueDate: datetime,
    knownRansomwareCampaignUse: string,
    notes: string)
    [@"https://www.cisa.gov/sites/default/files/csv/known_exploited_vulnerabilities.csv"]
    with (format="csv", ignoreFirstRecord=True);
MDETVM_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2d)
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    DeviceName = deviceName,
    CVEId = cveId,
    OsPlatform = osPlatform,
    OsVersion = osVersion,
    SoftwareName = softwareName,
    SoftwareVendor = softwareVendor,
    SoftwareVersion = softwareVersion,
    Severity = vulnerabilitySeverityLevel,
    CvssScore = cvssScore,
    ExploitabilityLevel = exploitabilityLevel,
    SecurityUpdateAvailable = securityUpdateAvailable,
    RecommendedUpdate = recommendedSecurityUpdate
| extend ExceptionKey = strcat(DeviceName, '|', CVEId)
| where ExceptionKey !in (ActiveExceptions)
| join kind=inner (CISA_KEV) on $left.CVEId == $right.cveID
| extend DueDateInDays = datetime_diff('day', dueDate, now())
| extend IsRansomware = knownRansomwareCampaignUse != "Unknown"
| extend IncidentTitle = strcat(CVEId, " - CISA KEV Detected on ", DeviceName)
| where IncidentTitle !in (PreviousIncidents)
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    DeviceName,
    CVEId,
    OsPlatform,
    OsVersion,
    SoftwareName,
    SoftwareVendor,
    SoftwareVersion,
    Severity,
    CvssScore,
    ExploitabilityLevel,
    SecurityUpdateAvailable,
    RecommendedUpdate,
    vendorProject,
    product,
    vulnerabilityName,
    dateAdded,
    requiredAction,
    dueDate,
    DueDateInDays,
    IsRansomware,
    knownRansomwareCampaignUse,
    shortDescription

alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: CVE **{{CVEId}}** was added to the CISA KEV catalog and is active on **{{DeviceName}}**. Check custom details for full context.
